summary: embed_ollama silently ignores batch_size — loops 1 HTTP request per chunk instead of batching

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: embed_corpus.py:194-211 outer loop slices chunks into batch_size groups (default 32 per config/embedding.toml), but embed_ollama (lines 35-49) ignores the array and does 'for text in texts: urlopen(...)' — one sequential HTTP round-trip per chunk. The response field is data['embeddings'] (plural) and the code only takes [0], suggesting the original author knew the API was batch-capable but didn't follow through. Current state: 2531 chunks took ~3-6 minutes when ~30 seconds is achievable with the same model on the same GPU.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content:
Fix is minimal — pass texts (the array) as 'input' instead of looping single strings. Ollama's /api/embed already accepts string OR array; response is array of arrays. New embed_ollama:

def embed_ollama(texts: list[str], model: str) -> list[list[float]]:
    payload = json.dumps({'model': model, 'input': texts}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        'http://localhost:11434/api/embed', data=payload,
        headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'})
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=120) as resp:
        return json.loads(resp.read())['embeddings']

Validation step before relying on it: embed two chunks individually, embed the same two as a batch, assert vectors are float-equal (modulo floating-point tolerance). This avoids a silent correctness regression in case ollama's batching path produces slightly different output than the per-call path. If outputs diverge meaningfully, fall back to per-call but increase HTTP keep-alive / use a session — even that beats the current shape. No re-embed needed if outputs match (same model, same vectors). corpus_metadata.embedding_model tag stays 'ollama/nomic-embed-text'.
